name: Pointe Est Du Recif Sud
description: This dive site in the Mozambique Channel features a stunning coral reef teeming with marine life. Expect vibrant hard and soft corals, schools of tropical fish, and frequent encounters with reef sharks and turtles. The underwater topography includes dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs, offering excellent visibility. Strong currents can occur, making it ideal for drift diving. The warm waters and rich biodiversity create an unforgettable experience for advanced divers.

name: Crayfish Alley
description: A vibrant coral garden teems with marine life along a gentle slope. Schools of tropical fish dart between colorful soft corals, while moray eels peer from crevices. The site's namesake crayfish are common, often spotted near the reef base. Visibility is excellent, with calm conditions ideal for beginners. The shallow depth and abundant sea life make it perfect for relaxed exploration.

name: Manta Reef
description: Manta Reef lies off Mozambique’s coast in the Indian Ocean, near Inhambane. This site is famous for its resident manta rays, often seen gliding through the clear waters. The reef features dramatic drop-offs and vibrant coral formations teeming with tropical fish. Strong currents attract pelagic species, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 30 meters. Divers may also encounter reef sharks, turtles, and large schools of barracuda. The site suits experienced divers due to occasional strong currents. A must-visit for those seeking big marine life encounters.

name: 5 Mile Reef
description: This vibrant reef stretches along the Mozambique Channel, teeming with marine life. Expect schools of tropical fish, turtles, and occasional reef sharks. The coral formations are healthy and diverse, with swim-throughs and overhangs to explore. Visibility is often excellent, reaching 30 meters. Currents can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. The site is best suited for intermediate to advanced divers. The warm Indian Ocean waters ensure comfortable diving year-round.

name: Lighthouse Reef
description: An underwater paradise off Maputo, this site features vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional turtles. The clear Indian Ocean waters offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 20 meters. Gentle currents make it ideal for divers of all levels. The reef slopes down to sandy patches where you might spot rays gliding by. Warm water temperatures year-round create comfortable diving conditions. The site's proximity to the coast allows for easy access while maintaining a pristine marine environment.

name: Sponge Garden
description: This vibrant dive site features a stunning sponge garden with colorful soft corals and abundant marine life. The shallow reef slopes gently, making it ideal for all skill levels. Expect to see schools of tropical fish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. The clear warm waters offer excellent visibility year-round. The site is known for its unique sponge formations and thriving coral ecosystem. Currents are mild, perfect for relaxed exploration. A must-visit for underwater photographers and macro enthusiasts.

name: La Cheminee De Brandele
description: This dive site features a dramatic underwater chimney, a volcanic formation teeming with marine life. Schools of barracuda and trevally patrol the area, while reef sharks often cruise by. The walls are covered in colorful soft corals and sponges. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich water, attracting large pelagics. Visibility is typically excellent, often exceeding 30 meters. Depths range from 15 to 40 meters, suitable for advanced divers. The site sits in the Mozambique Channel near Mayotte, known for pristine coral reefs.

name: Hotel Reef
description: This vibrant reef sits just off Inhaca Island in Mozambique's warm Indian Ocean waters. The site features a sloping coral garden teeming with colorful fish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners, while the 12-18-meter depth offers great visibility. The highlight is an old shipwreck covered in soft corals, attracting schools of tropical fish. Expect encounters with turtles, rays, and seasonal whale sharks. The shallow sections near the island's edge are perfect for snorkelers. Water temperatures stay warm year-round, with best conditions from April to November.

name: Barra Lagoon
description: This sheltered lagoon in Mozambique's Inhambane region offers calm, clear waters perfect for beginners. The sandy bottom slopes gently to 12 meters, dotted with vibrant soft corals and sea fans. Expect frequent sightings of reef fish, octopus, and occasional rays gliding over the seabed. The warm Indian Ocean currents bring excellent visibility year-round, with minimal surge. A great spot for relaxed diving and underwater photography, especially during the tide changes when marine life becomes more active.

name: Salon
description: This dive site in Mozambique's Inhambane region features vibrant coral reefs teeming with marine life. Expect encounters with reef sharks, turtles, and large schools of tropical fish. The clear waters of the Indian Ocean offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 20 meters. The underwater topography includes gentle slopes and occasional drop-offs, suitable for divers of various experience levels. Currents are typically mild, making it ideal for relaxed exploration. The warm water temperatures create comfortable diving conditions year-round.
